verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type I transmembrane protein that stably reaches the sperm plasma membrane outer face following acrosome reaction and capacitation—documented physiological conditions required for fertilization. In uncapacitated/unactivated sperm, IZUMO1 is localized to the acrosomal membrane interior. Upon acrosome reaction in the female reproductive tract, IZUMO1 translocates to and remains stably anchored on the sperm cell surface plasma membrane as an essential gamete adhesion and fusion ligand. The protein itself (not transient recruitment) provides the surface anchoring via its transmembrane domain, qualifying it as contextually surface-accessible.
